mirror_convention: Each HTML page at <name>.html has a Markdown mirror at <name>.md served from the same directory.

pages.title: Overview
pages.summary: Entry point for xvn operator docs. Covers what the system does, the safety model, and a five-minute local backtest quickstart. Links to manual, CLI reference, and architecture pages.

pages.title: Operator manual
pages.summary: Operator-side prerequisites that block specific milestones. Four tiers, sixteen milestones. CV vector-extraction tasks (M1-M4) retired with ADR 0011. Surviving tiers: Tier 2 forward-paper and on-chain, Tier 3 secrets and models and datasets, Tier 4 non-blocking research and upstream. Includes runbook entries for dashboard auth and observability.

pages.title: CLI reference
pages.summary: Reference for the xvn binary. Sixteen top-level verbs. Argv-only contract — no shell text, no caller-controlled cwd, no caller-controlled env in v1. Strategy authoring writes ULID bundles. Eval runs persist to SQLite at $XVN_HOME/xvn.db. Remote CLI runs through Tailscale-served dashboard, not arbitrary SSH.

exit_codes.1: user — bad argv, validation failure, missing required env
exit_codes.2: notfound — strategy ULID, scenario id, or run id not found
exit_codes.3: provider — LLM provider non-2xx or unparseable response after retry
exit_codes.4: venue — broker rejected order, venue unreachable, portfolio query failed
exit_codes.5: risk — risk layer vetoed every candidate decision in the run
exit_codes.10: internal — bug. Capture the run, file an issue.

pages.title: Architecture
pages.summary: Four-stage pipeline (Intern, Trader, Risk, Execution) with two named LLM roles. Risk layer is deterministic code, no model in the loop. Eval framework has a pre-committed primary metric (Delta-Sharpe). ERC-8004 identity is opt-in and gated by ADR 0008.

stages.name: Stage 2 · Trader
stages.role: LLM judgment on briefing, wrapped as TraderArm

stages.name: Risk layer
stages.role: deterministic veto/downsize/passthrough, never increases size or flips direction

do_not: Build prod images on deploy hosts
Assume arbitrary SSH on live nodes
Run dashboard or mcp or fire-trade via the remote-CLI job API
Treat the Intern as a recommender — it emits balanced evidence only
